title: gonocarpus micranthus subsp. micranthus
intro: Gonocarpus micranthus subsp. micranthus is a subspecies of plant in the family Haloragaceae, commonly known as the small watermilfoil or tiny watermilfoil. It is a perennial aquatic plant native to Australia and New Zealand, known for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues. The plant contains compounds with anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of coughs, colds, and bronchitis, providing relief to those suffering from respiratory problems. The leaves and stems of the plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, making it a valuable herbal remedy in traditional medicine.
